name: F-35 Lightning II
summary: The F-35 Lightning II is a 5th Generation fighter, combining advanced stealth with fighter speed and agility, fully fused sensor information, network-enabled operations and advanced sustainment.

name: HIMARS
summary: The M142 HIMARS is a light multiple rocket launcher developed in the late 1990s for the United States Army, mounted on a standard Army M1140 truck frame.

name: PAC-3 MSE
summary: The PAC-3 Missile Segment Enhancement (MSE) is a high-velocity, hit-to-kill surface-to-air missile that provides advanced firepower to the Patriot air defense system.

name: Javelin
summary: Javelin is a premier man-portable, fire-and-forget anti-tank missile system. It's highly effective against modern tanks and light armored vehicles.

name: C-130J Super Hercules
summary: The C-130J Super Hercules is the most advanced C-130 ever designed, built and flown. It is the tactical airlifter of choice for 22 nations and 26 operators.

name: Aegis Combat System
summary: The Aegis Combat System is a centralized, automated, command-and-control and weapons control system that is the heart of the U.S. Navy's surface fleet.

name: CH-53K King Stallion
summary: The CH-53K King Stallion is the most powerful helicopter ever built by the United States. It is designed to lift extreme loads over long distances in high/hot conditions.

name: LRASM
summary: The Long Range Anti-Ship Missile (LRASM) is a precision-guided, intelligent anti-ship missile designed to meet the needs of U.S. Navy and Air Force warfighters.

name: SR-71 Blackbird
summary: The SR-71 Blackbird remains the world's fastest and highest-flying air-breathing manned aircraft. Developed by Skunk Works, it could outrun missiles.

name: F-22 Raptor
summary: The F-22 Raptor defines air dominance. Its combination of stealth, speed, agility, and integrated avionics enables it to deliver lethal capabilities in any environment.

name: Orion Spacecraft
summary: Lockheed Martin is building the Orion spacecraft for NASA’s Artemis program, designed to carry astronauts to the Moon and beyond to Mars.

name: THAAD Weapon System
summary: Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) is a highly effective, combat-proven defense against short, medium, and intermediate-range ballistic missile threats.

name: Autonomous BLACK HAWK
summary: In 2022, Lockheed Martin and DARPA completed the first-ever pilotless BLACK HAWK flight, demonstrating the future of autonomous battlefield logistics.

name: F-117 Nighthawk
summary: The F-117 Nighthawk was the world's first operational stealth aircraft. Its 'faceting' design paved the way for all future stealth platforms.

name: Viking Mars Lander
summary: In 1976, the Viking 1 and 2 landers, built by Martin Marietta, became the first U.S. spacecraft to successfully land on Mars and return high-resolution images.

name: Founding of Skunk Works
summary: In 1943, Kelly Johnson founded the Advanced Development Programs (Skunk Works) in a circus tent. It has since developed the world's most advanced aircraft.

name: The First Packed Parachute
summary: In 1913, Glenn L. Martin invented the first parachute to be packed into a backpack-style container, revolutionizing aviation safety.

name: Sinking of the Ostfriesland
summary: In 1921, Glenn Martin’s MB-2 bombers proved the power of air supremacy by successfully sinking the captured German battleship Ostfriesland.

name: Hellfire Missile
summary: The AGM-114 Hellfire is a combat-proven air-to-ground precision missile used on helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft for anti-armor and precision strikes.

name: JASSM
summary: The Joint Air-to-Surface Standoff Missile (JASSM) is a long-range, precision-guided cruise missile for penetrating enemy air defenses from standoff distances.

name: AGM-179 JAGM
summary: The Joint Air-to-Ground Missile (JAGM) is a multi-mode precision strike missile replacing legacy systems for rotary- and fixed-wing platforms.

name: GMLRS
summary: Guided Multiple Launch Rocket System (GMLRS) provides precision long-range fires for HIMARS and MLRS platforms, with extended range variants in development.

name: ATACMS
summary: Army Tactical Missile System (ATACMS) is a surface-to-surface missile delivering precision strikes against high-value targets at extended ranges.

name: PrSM
summary: Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) is the next-generation surface-to-surface missile for HIMARS, offering increased range, lethality, and survivability.

name: U-2 Dragon Lady
summary: The U-2 high-altitude reconnaissance aircraft, developed by Skunk Works, provides critical intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance capabilities.

name: P-3 Orion
summary: The P-3 Orion maritime patrol aircraft excels in anti-submarine warfare, surveillance, and search-and-rescue missions worldwide.

name: UH-60 Black Hawk
summary: The UH-60 Black Hawk is the U.S. Army's primary utility helicopter, renowned for troop transport, medical evacuation, and assault missions.

name: Sikorsky S-92
summary: The S-92 helicopter serves military and civil roles, including search-and-rescue, offshore transport, and executive transport with proven reliability.

name: F-16 Fighting Falcon
summary: The F-16 Fighting Falcon is a versatile multirole fighter with over 4,600 built, serving dozens of air forces worldwide.

name: First Flight of the U-2
summary: In 1955, the U-2 spy plane took its first flight, enabling high-altitude reconnaissance during the Cold War.

name: Apollo Program Contribution
summary: Lockheed (via heritage companies) contributed to NASA's Apollo program, including propulsion and systems for lunar missions.

name: Trident II D5 Missile
summary: The Trident II D5 is a submarine-launched ballistic missile providing strategic nuclear deterrence for the U.S. Navy.

name: LONGBOW Fire Control
summary: LONGBOW provides advanced fire control and radar for the AH-64 Apache helicopter, enhancing targeting in all conditions.

name: Space Fence
summary: The Space Fence system tracks objects in orbit for space situational awareness and national security.

name: First Autonomous F-35 Flight Demo
summary: Lockheed Martin demonstrated AI and autonomy integrations on the F-35, advancing manned-unmanned teaming.

name: Directed Energy Laser Systems
summary: Lockheed Martin's high-energy laser systems provide counter-drone and precision defense capabilities.

name: MQ-25 Stingray Contribution
summary: Lockheed Martin supports unmanned aerial refueling systems like elements of the MQ-25 program.

name: Hypersonic Strike Weapon Development
summary: Lockheed Martin advances hypersonic weapons like ARRW for rapid, long-range precision strike.

name: First Flight of the F-117
summary: In 1981, the F-117 Nighthawk's first flight marked the dawn of operational stealth aviation.

name: Blackbird's Mach 3+ Record
summary: In 1976, the SR-71 set an absolute speed record of Mach 3.3+ that still stands for air-breathing aircraft.

name: First Orion Test Flight (EFT-1)
summary: In 2014, the Orion spacecraft's Exploration Flight Test-1 successfully demonstrated reentry and recovery.

name: Skunk Works' Have Blue Prototype
summary: In 1977, the Have Blue demonstrator proved low-observable technology, leading to the F-117.

name: Pave Low MH-53 Helicopter Legacy
summary: Heritage Sikorsky MH-53 Pave Low helicopters enabled special operations deep behind enemy lines.

name: First Production F-35 Delivery
summary: In 2011, the first operational F-35 was delivered, ushering in 5th-generation fighter era for multiple nations.

name: Aegis Ashore Deployment
summary: Aegis Ashore systems extend naval missile defense to land-based sites for ballistic missile threats.

name: Viking 1 First Mars Photo
summary: In 1976, Viking 1 transmitted the first color images from Mars' surface, a landmark in planetary exploration.

name: EADGE-T C2 Program
summary: The Enterprise Air Defense Ground Environment – Transformation (EADGE-T) provides the UAE with a state-of-the-art, integrated command and control system.

name: L-1011 TriStar
summary: The L-1011 TriStar was a technologically advanced wide-body trijet airliner, often called the 'most intelligent airliner ever built'.

name: P-51 Mustang
summary: The P-51 Mustang, while originally a North American Aviation design, shares a historical legacy with Lockheed Martin's heritage companies in the defense industry's evolution.

name: Polaris SLBM
summary: The UGM-27 Polaris was the first submarine-launched ballistic missile, revolutionizing strategic nuclear deterrence.

name: SBIRS Satellite
summary: The Space-Based Infrared System (SBIRS) provides critical missile warning and surveillance capabilities from space.

name: C2BMC
summary: Command, Control, Battle Management, and Communications (C2BMC) is the 'brain' of the U.S. Ballistic Missile Defense System.

name: Presidential Helicopter
summary: Lockheed Martin and Sikorsky build the VH-92A, the next-generation helicopter for the President of the United States.

name: UH-60 Black Hawk
summary: The Sikorsky UH-60 Black Hawk is the most widely used multi-mission helicopter in the world, with over 4,000 aircraft in service with dozens of nations.
